item.title: ChatGPT遭遇强烈反弹，揭示与特朗普结盟的新隐患
item.description:
在与五角大楼的争端中，竞争对手AI工具Claude的下载量激增。

【原文：

Downloads of rival AI tool Claude soar amid feud with the Pentagon.

】

item.title: 奈飞收购本·阿弗莱克创立的人工智能电影制作公司
item.description: 网飞公司收购了由演员本·阿弗莱克四年前创立的人工智能电影制作技术公司InterPositive。【原文：Netflix Inc. acquired InterPositive, an AI filmmaking technology business founded four years ago by actor Ben Affleck.】

item.title: 巴西央行官员暗示利率路径可能因伊朗战争而改变
item.description: 巴西央行一位关键决策者周四表示，该行准备在本月晚些时候启动宽松周期之际，"无法忽视"伊朗战争带来的经济影响。【原文：Brazil’s central bank “can’t ignore” the economic fallout of the war in Iran as it prepares to start an easing cycle later this month, one of bank’s key policymakers said Thursday.】

item.title: 摩根大通3500人规模的杠杆融资活动因战争现实冲击而受挫
item.description: 当数千名银行家、投资者和分析师齐聚迈阿密海滩参加摩根大通公司年度会议——该领域规模最大的行业盛会时，价值约3万亿美元的杠杆融资市场已弥漫着不安情绪。【原文：There was already a sense of unease about the roughly $3 trillion leveraged finance market as thousands of bankers, investors and analysts descended on Miami Beach for JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s annual confab, the biggest of its kind.】

item.title: 世界银行计划斥资5亿美元升级肯尼亚通勤铁路系统
item.description: 世界银行已拨出5亿美元专项资金，用于修复肯尼亚首都的通勤铁路网络。【原文：The World Bank has earmarked $500 million to rehabilitate the commuter rail network in Kenya’s capital.】

item.title: 亚马逊押注医疗健康人工智能，推出面向患者和医生的工具
item.description: 亚马逊公司旗下云计算部门正推出面向医疗实践的人工智能工具，这是该公司最新举措，旨在将云计算业务的触角延伸至职场应用领域。【原文：Amazon.com Inc.’s cloud unit is launching AI tools for medical practices, the company’s latest effort to broaden the reach of its cloud-computing business into workplace applications.】

item.title: 伯克希尔暂无处置卡夫亨氏股份计划，因拆分计划已暂停
item.description: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司首席执行官格雷格·阿贝尔表示，在食品公司卡夫亨氏暂停分拆计划后，这家综合企业集团目前没有改变其在卡夫亨氏公司持股比例的计划。【原文：Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Chief Executive Officer Greg Abel said the conglomerate has no immediate plans to alter its stake in Kraft Heinz Co. after the food company paused its plans to split in two.】

item.title: 巴金称美联储对伊朗战争的反应取决于冲击持续时间
item.description: 里士满联邦储备银行行长汤姆·巴金表示，美国央行对美以与伊朗战争的应对措施将取决于其对美国经济的影响持续多久。【原文：Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin said the US central bank’s response to the US-Israel war with Iran will depend on how long the impact on the US economy lasts.】

item.title: 脑科技初创企业Science Corp.筹集2.3亿美元用于治疗失明
item.description: 神经科技公司Science Corp.从投资者处筹集了2.3亿美元资金，用于将其治疗失明的植入装置商业化，并开发更先进的大脑设备。【原文：Neurotechnology company Science Corp. raised $230 million from investors to commercialize its implant for blindness and develop even more advanced brain devices.】

item.title: 德国复兴信贷银行据称正与摩根大通洽谈潜在KNDS股权交易
item.description: 知情人士透露，德国国家开发银行正与摩根大通合作，拟收购荷兰KNDS公司部分股权。此举表明柏林方面正推进计划，以确保在该法德合资坦克制造商中掌握影响力——该公司计划于今年夏季上市。【原文：State-owned lender KfW is working with JPMorgan Chase & Co. on the potential purchase of a stake in KNDS NV, people familiar with the matter said, in a sign that Berlin is moving ahead with plans to secure influence in the Franco-German tankmaker that’s looking to go public this summer.】

item.title: 美国生产率超预期增长，企业积极控制成本
item.description: 美国第四季度劳动生产率增幅超出预期，此前该指标已创下五年来的最大涨幅，这进一步证明企业正努力提高效率以降低成本。【原文：US labor productivity rose in the fourth quarter by more than forecast after the strongest advance in five years, adding to evidence that companies are striving for greater efficiency to contain costs.】

item.title: 美国初请失业金人数维持低位，显示劳动力市场趋稳
item.description: 上周美国失业救济金申请人数持平，进一步印证劳动力市场正在趋于稳定。【原文：Applications for US unemployment benefits held steady last week, adding to evidence that the labor market is stabilizing.】

item.title: EQT以5.75亿美元收购枫树地产旗下东海岸仓库
item.description: 丰树投资私人有限公司同意以5.75亿新元将一组东海岸仓库出售给EQT房地产公司。【原文：Mapletree Investments Pte agreed to sell a portfolio of East Coast warehouses to EQT Real Estate for $575 million.】

item.title: 美国国防部寻求信息以扩大金属储备
item.description: 美国国防部正寻求有关五种关键矿物的储备信息，该国正加大力度降低供应链风险并加强国内原料保障。【原文：The US Defense Department is seeking information for the potential stockpiling of five critical minerals, as the country steps up efforts to reduce risks in the supply chain and bolster domestic feedstock.】

item.title: 波兰央行行长考虑出售黄金为国防筹资
item.description: 据知情人士透露，波兰央行行长提出一项计划，拟通过出售黄金储备筹集高达480亿兹罗提（约合130亿美元）资金用于国防开支，该计划已获得波兰总统支持。【原文：Poland’s central bank chief laid out a proposal to generate as much as 48 billion zloty ($13 billion) from the sale of gold reserves to finance defense spending as part of a plan backed by the nation’s president, according to people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: 伊朗战争为俄罗斯重建储备提供了短暂窗口期
item.description: 由美以对伊朗开战引发的油价飙升虽无法解决克里姆林宫的预算问题，但可能帮助俄罗斯补充迅速耗尽的应急储备金。【原文：A surge in oil prices driven by the US-Israel war on Iran won’t solve the Kremlin’s budget problem, but it could help Russia replenish rapidly depleting rainy-day reserves.】

item.title: 英国抵押贷款即将涨价，因伊朗战争推高掉期利率
item.description: 一项反映英国未来利率路径预期的关键指标已升至今年最高水平，加剧了人们对抵押贷款成本再度上涨的担忧。【原文：A key gauge that reflects expectations for the future path of UK interest rates has risen to the highest this year, fueling fears over a fresh increase in mortgage costs.】

item.title: 创始人基金领投8000万美元押注制造业独角兽企业
item.description: Founders Fund正牵头对Nominal公司进行8000万美元的投资，这家初创企业致力于实现工厂现代化，提升制造业效率。【原文：Founders Fund is leading an $80 million investment in Nominal Inc., a startup aiming to modernize factories and make manufacturing more efficient.】

item.title: 欧洲央行行长纳格尔称，伊朗战争引发的通胀问题比经济增长更令人担忧
item.description: 欧洲央行理事会成员约阿希姆·纳格尔表示，在评估伊朗战争的影响时，通胀问题比经济增长更令人担忧。【原文：Inflation is a bigger concern than economic growth as the European Central Bank assesses the implications of the war in Iran, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.】

item.title: 企业直面新H-1B签证规则下的十万美元费用
item.description: 特朗普政府针对高技能移民的美国签证体系改革即将迎来首次重大考验。【原文：The Trump administration’s overhaul of the US visa system for highly skilled immigrants is about to get its first major test.】

item.title: 纽约证券交易所母公司以250亿美元估值投资加密货币交易所
item.description: 洲际交易所集团正收购OKX的股权，该交易对这家加密货币交易所运营商的估值为250亿美元。【原文：Intercontinental Exchange Inc. is acquiring a stake in OKX in a deal that values the cryptocurrency exchange operator at $25 billion.】

item.title: 塞拉太空公司融资5.5亿美元，估值升至80亿美元
item.description: 塞拉太空公司已筹集5.5亿美元资金，助力其成为美国政府国防承包商的计划。【原文：Sierra Space Corp. has raised $550 million, fueling its drive to become a defense contractor for the US government.】

item.title: 美国官员称不会允许印度成为像中国那样的竞争对手
item.description: 美国副国务卿克里斯托弗·兰道周四表示，美国不会给予印度与中国同等的经济优惠待遇——正是这些优惠使中国崛起为主要竞争对手。此番表态表明华盛顿在贸易协定谈判中持谨慎态度。【原文：The US won’t give India the same kind of economic advantages it gave China, which allowed that country to emerge as a major competitor, Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau said on Thursday, signaling Washington’s cautiousness in negotiations over a trade deal.】

item.title: 能源主导地位无法阻止美国燃料成本持续攀升
item.description: 作为全球第一大钻井国，仍不足以使该国免受战争的影响。【原文：Being the world’s No. 1 driller isn’t enough to shield the country from the effects of the war.】

item.title: 美国盘前活跃股：博通、赛纳、Stubhub、Trade Desk、Veeva
item.description: 截至纽约时间上午7点45分，标普500指数期货基本持平，中东战事再度推高油价。【原文：S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as the war in the Middle East again sends oil higher.】

item.title: 津巴布韦总统称该国正采取措施清偿230亿美元债务
item.description: 津巴布韦总统埃默森·姆南加古瓦表示，随着与贷款方的谈判进展顺利，津巴布韦正采取措施清偿欠多边机构的230亿美元债务。【原文：Zimbabwe is taking steps to clear the $23 billion it owes multilateral institutions, as talks with lenders progress well, President Emmerson Mnangagwa said.】

item.title: 普拉达与范思哲合并将稀释利润率
item.description: 普拉达集团公布的2025财年业绩符合预期，同时向投资者表示，随着这家意大利奢侈品集团致力于扭转局面，其新收购的范思哲品牌将稀释利润率。【原文：Prada SpA reported 2025 results that met expectations, while telling investors its newly acquired Versace brand will dilute profit margins as the Italian luxury group works to turn it around.】

item.title: 斯蒂夫尔称维斯特里股价暴跌后成为"不容错过的超值股"
item.description: 本周英国住宅建筑商维斯特里集团股价暴跌，使其股价已低廉到不容忽视。【原文：This week’s plunge in UK homebuilder Vistry Group Plc has left the shares too cheap to ignore.】

item.title: 印度要求美国就霍尔木兹海峡保险计划作出明确说明
item.description: 据一位熟悉此事的政府官员透露，印度已与美国举行会谈，旨在就为穿越霍尔木兹海峡的油轮及其他船舶提供保险的拟议机制寻求明确说明。【原文：India has held talks with the US to seek clarity on a proposed mechanism to provide insurance for oil tankers and other vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to a government official familiar with the matter.】

item.title: 美国裁员公告在2026年初激增后趋缓
item.description: 美国企业裁员公告在上月从1月的激增中回落，这进一步表明劳动力市场正在趋于稳定。【原文：Layoff announcements at US companies subsided last month from a January surge, adding to signs of stabilization in the labor market.】
